title: C002: Add container image CI workflow
description:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** S007

## Summary

Create `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` implementing S007 from the project plan. The workflow builds OpenStack service container images (starting with Keystone) as multi-arch builds (`linux/amd64`, `linux/arm64`) and pushes them to GHCR. Base images (`python-base`, `venv-builder`) are built as prerequisite jobs within the same workflow and passed to service builds via `--build-context` overrides pointing at `docker-image://ghcr.io/...` refs. The workflow uses a matrix strategy over services and releases, resolves upstream source refs from `releases/<release>/source-refs.yaml` (currently `keystone: "28.0.0"`), applies patches and constraint overrides conditionally, and tags images with composite format `<service>:<upstream-version>-p<N>-<branch>-<sha>` plus standalone `<upstream-version>` and `<sha>` tags. A smoke test job validates the built image by running `keystone-manage --version`.

On PRs, base images are always pushed to GHCR (small, shared, required for `docker-image://` build-context resolution), while service images build single-arch (`linux/amd64`) with `--load` for local smoke testing without registry push. On push events, all images build multi-arch and push to GHCR.

## Scope

**Included:**
- Workflow file `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` with SPDX header matching `ci.yaml` convention (`Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company`, Apache-2.0), quoted `"on"` key, top-level `permissions:` block, and three jobs
- All jobs use `runs-on: ubuntu-latest` matching `ci.yaml` convention; `timeout-minutes` set per job (base and service builds need higher than `ci.yaml`'s 10 min — 30 min recommended for multi-arch builds)
- `build-base-images` job: sequential build of `python-base` (from `images/python-base/`, base `ubuntu:noble`) then `venv-builder` (from `images/venv-builder/`, with `--build-context python-base=docker-image://...`); always multi-arch; always push to GHCR (including PRs — required for `docker-image://` refs in service builds); outputs image refs with digest (`ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base@sha256:...`) for downstream jobs
- `build-service-images` job (matrix over `service: [keystone]` × `release: ["2025.2"]`): resolves ref from `releases/<release>/source-refs.yaml` via `yq` (`.keystone` yields `"28.0.0"`); checks out `openstack/<service>` at resolved ref into `src/<service>`; applies patches conditionally with `git -C src/<service> apply patches/<service>/<release>/*.patch` (guarded by `hashFiles` — currently no `patches/` directory exists, step is no-op); runs `scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh <release>` (idempotent, exits 0 when `overrides/<release>/constraints.txt` absent); builds with `docker/build-push-action`
- **PR behavior**: single-arch `linux/amd64` with `load: true` (image available locally for smoke test), `push: false`
- **Push behavior**: multi-arch `linux/amd64,linux/arm64` with `push: true`
- Four `--build-context` overrides for service builds: `python-base=docker-image://ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base@sha256:...`, `venv-builder=docker-image://ghcr.io/c5c3/venv-builder@sha256:...`, `<service>=src/<service>`, `upper-constraints=releases/<release>/`
- Multi-arch via `docker/setup-buildx-action` + `docker/build-push-action` for `linux/amd64,linux/arm64`
- GHCR login with `docker/login-action` using `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Tag schema: composite `<upstream-version>-p<N>-<branch>-<sha>` (e.g., `keystone:28.0.0-p0-main-a1b2c3d`), plus standalone `<upstream-version>` tag (e.g., `keystone:28.0.0`) and `<sha>` tag (e.g., `keystone:a1b2c3d`)
- Patch count derivation: `p0` when no `patches/<service>/<release>/` directory or no `.patch` files; `pN` = count of `.patch` files
- Branch name sanitization for Docker tag compatibility (replace `/` with `-`, e.g., `stable/2025.2` → `stable-2025.2`)
- `smoke-test` job: depends on `build-service-images`; on PRs uses locally loaded image, on push pulls from GHCR; runs `docker run --rm <image> keystone-manage --version`; non-zero exit fails the workflow
- Triggers: `push` to `main` and `stable/**` branches, `pull_request`
- GHA layer caching (`cache-from: type=gha`, `cache-to: type=gha,mode=max`)
- Concurrency control matching `ci.yaml` pattern (`group: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }}`, `cancel-in-progress` on PR only)
- Actions pinned to full commit SHAs with version comments (matching `ci.yaml` convention, e.g., `actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6`)
- Permissions: top-level `contents: read`, `packages: write` (top-level block matching `ci.yaml` convention — `packages: write` added for GHCR push)

**Excluded:**
- SBOM generation and attestation (`architecture/docs/08-container-images/04-sbom.md` documents CycloneDX via `anchore/sbom-action` + `actions/attest-sbom` — separate feature requiring `id-token: write` and `attestations: write` permissions)
- Renovate custom regex managers for `source-refs.yaml` (`architecture/docs/08-container-images/02-versioning.md` documents planned config — separate concern, current `renovate.json` only has `config:recommended`)
- Operator image builds (S017/S018 — separate scope, different Dockerfiles in `operators/`)
- Helm chart packaging and push (S018 scope)
- Library patch checkout and build-context injection (Level 2 patches from `architecture/docs/08-container-images/03-patching.md` — no library patches exist yet, YAGNI)
- OVN/OVS build pipeline (architecture docs mark as TODO)
- E2E or integration testing beyond the smoke test (separate workflow scope)
- Adding new services beyond keystone to the matrix (matrix is extensible, but only `images/keystone/Dockerfile` exists today)
- `docker-build` Makefile target update (currently a stub error referencing S006 — can be updated separately)
- `extra-packages.yaml` consumption (declarative config consumed by Dockerfiles directly, not by the workflow)

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph Triggers
        PR["Pull Request"]
        Push["Push main / stable/**"]
    end

    subgraph BaseJob["Job: build-base-images"]
        BCheckout["Checkout repo"]
        BBuildx["Setup Buildx"]
        BLogin["Login to GHCR"]
        BB1["Build + push python-base"]
        BB2["Build + push venv-builder"]
        BCheckout --> BBuildx --> BLogin --> BB1 --> BB2
    end

    subgraph Matrix["Job: build-service-images #40;matrix: service x release#41;"]
        Checkout["Checkout C5C3 repo"]
        Resolve["Resolve source ref #40;yq .keystone#41;"]
        CheckoutSrc["Checkout openstack/keystone at ref"]
        Patches["Apply patches #40;conditional, currently no-op#41;"]
        Overrides["Apply constraint overrides #40;idempotent#41;"]
        PatchCount["Derive patch count #40;pN#41;"]
        TagCalc["Calculate tags"]
        Buildx["Setup Docker Buildx"]
        Login["Login to GHCR"]
        Build["Build image with 4 build-contexts"]
        PushDecision{"PR?"}
        LoadLocal["load: true #40;amd64 only#41;"]
        PushGHCR["push: true #40;amd64+arm64#41;"]

        Checkout --> Resolve --> CheckoutSrc --> Patches --> Overrides --> PatchCount --> TagCalc --> Buildx --> Login --> Build --> PushDecision
        PushDecision -->|yes| LoadLocal
        PushDecision -->|no| PushGHCR
    end

    subgraph Smoke["Job: smoke-test"]
        Pull["Load or pull built image"]
        RunTest["docker run keystone-manage --version"]
        Pull --> RunTest
    end

    PR --> BaseJob
    Push --> BaseJob
    BaseJob --> Matrix
    Matrix --> Smoke
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph BuildContexts["Named Build Contexts"]
        BC1["python-base = docker-image://ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base@sha256:..."]
        BC2["venv-builder = docker-image://ghcr.io/c5c3/venv-builder@sha256:..."]
        BC3["keystone = src/keystone"]
        BC4["upper-constraints = releases/2025.2/"]
    end

    subgraph Dockerfile["images/keystone/Dockerfile"]
        S1["FROM venv-builder AS build"]
        S2["RUN uv pip install --constraint ... keystone"]
        S3["FROM python-base"]
        S4["COPY --from=build /var/lib/openstack"]
    end

    subgraph Tags["Image Tags #40;3 per service#41;"]
        T1["keystone:28.0.0-p0-main-a1b2c3d"]
        T2["keystone:28.0.0"]
        T3["keystone:a1b2c3d"]
    end

    BC1 --> S3
    BC2 --> S1
    BC3 --> S2
    BC4 --> S2
    Dockerfile --> Tags --> GHCR["ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone"]
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant GHA as GitHub Actions
    participant YQ as yq
    participant Git as git
    participant Script as apply-constraint-overrides.sh
    participant Buildx as Docker Buildx
    participant GHCR as ghcr.io

    GHA->>YQ: Read releases/2025.2/source-refs.yaml
    YQ-->>GHA: keystone = "28.0.0"
    GHA->>Git: checkout openstack/keystone@28.0.0 into src/keystone
    GHA->>Git: apply patches/keystone/2025.2/*.patch #40;if any#41;
    GHA->>Script: apply-constraint-overrides.sh 2025.2
    Script-->>GHA: constraints updated #40;or no-op#41;
    GHA->>GHA: Derive tags #40;28.0.0-p0-main-a1b2c3d, 28.0.0, a1b2c3d#41;
    GHA->>Buildx: Build with 4 build-contexts
    alt Push event
        Buildx->>GHCR: Push ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone #40;multi-arch#41;
        GHA->>GHCR: Pull image for smoke test
    else PR event
        Buildx->>GHA: Load image locally #40;amd64 only#41;
    end
    GHA->>GHA: docker run keystone-manage --version
```

## Key Components

- **`.github/workflows/build-images.yaml`**: Workflow file with SPDX header, quoted `"on"` key, triggers (`push: [main, stable/**]` + `pull_request`), concurrency control (matching `ci.yaml`: `group: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }}`, cancel-in-progress on PR), top-level permissions (`contents: read`, `packages: write`), three jobs on `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`
- **`build-base-images` job**: Sequential build of `python-base` (from `images/python-base/`, base `ubuntu:noble`) then `venv-builder` (from `images/venv-builder/`, with `--build-context python-base=docker-image://...`); always multi-arch; always pushed to GHCR (even on PRs — required for `docker-image://` refs in downstream service builds); outputs image refs with digest for downstream consumption; `timeout-minutes: 30`
- **`build-service-images` job (matrix)**: Matrix over `service: [keystone]` × `release: ["2025.2"]`; resolves ref from `releases/<release>/source-refs.yaml` via `yq` (`.keystone` yields `"28.0.0"`); checks out `openstack/<service>` at that ref into `src/<service>`; applies patches with `git -C src/<service> apply patches/<service>/<release>/*.patch` (conditional on `hashFiles` — currently no patches exist, step is no-op); runs `scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh <release>` (idempotent, exits 0 when `overrides/<release>/constraints.txt` absent); builds with `docker/build-push-action` passing four `build-contexts`; on push: multi-arch with three tags; on PR: single-arch `amd64` with `--load`; `timeout-minutes: 30`
- **Tag derivation step**: Reads upstream version from `source-refs.yaml` via `yq`; counts `.patch` files in `patches/<service>/<release>/` (defaulting to `p0` when directory absent); sanitizes branch name (`${GITHUB_REF_NAME//\//-}`); extracts 7-char short SHA; assembles composite `<version>-p<N>-<branch>-<sha>` plus standalone `<version>` and `<sha>` tags
- **`smoke-test` job**: Depends on `build-service-images`; on PRs uses locally loaded image, on push pulls from GHCR; runs `docker run --rm <image> keystone-manage --version`; non-zero exit fails the workflow; `timeout-minutes: 10`
- **Action version pinning**: All third-party actions pinned to full commit SHAs with version comments matching `ci.yaml` convention (e.g., `actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6`, `docker/build-push-action@<sha> # v6`)

stories.title: CI pipeline builds base images as prerequisites for service images
stories.want: the build-images workflow to build python-base and venv-builder sequentially and push them to GHCR before service builds start
stories.so_that: service image builds can reference base images via docker-image:// build-context URIs without manual pre-publishing
criteria: build-base-images job builds python-base first, then venv-builder with --build-context python-base=docker-image://... pointing at the just-pushed python-base
Both base images are always pushed to GHCR (including on PRs) because downstream docker-image:// refs require registry availability
build-base-images job outputs image refs with digest (ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base@sha256:...) consumed by build-service-images via needs.build-base-images.outputs
Base images are built multi-arch (linux/amd64, linux/arm64) on all trigger events
build-base-images job has timeout-minutes: 30

stories.title: CI pipeline builds service images with matrix strategy
stories.want: service images to be built via a matrix over service x release, resolving upstream source refs, applying patches and constraint overrides, and passing four named build-contexts
stories.so_that: adding new services or releases requires only matrix and config changes, not workflow restructuring
criteria: build-service-images job uses matrix: { service: [keystone], release: ['2025.2'] }
Source ref resolved from releases/<release>/source-refs.yaml via yq (e.g., .keystone yields '28.0.0')
openstack/<service> checked out at the resolved ref into src/<service>
Patches applied conditionally with git -C src/<service> apply patches/<service>/<release>/*.patch guarded by hashFiles
scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh <release> invoked (idempotent, exits 0 when no overrides exist)
Four build-contexts passed: python-base=docker-image://..., venv-builder=docker-image://..., <service>=src/<service>, upper-constraints=releases/<release>/

stories.title: PR builds are fast and provide local smoke testing
stories.want: PR builds to use single-arch (linux/amd64) with --load for local image availability without pushing service images to GHCR
stories.so_that: PR CI is fast and does not pollute the registry with unmerged image tags
criteria: On pull_request events, service images build with platforms: linux/amd64, load: true, push: false
On push events, service images build with platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64, push: true
Base images are always pushed (even on PRs) since docker-image:// refs require registry presence
Smoke test job can access the locally loaded image on PRs without pulling from GHCR

stories.title: Push builds produce tagged multi-arch images in GHCR
stories.want: push-to-main and push-to-stable builds to produce multi-arch images with three distinct tags per service
stories.so_that: deployment systems can reference images by upstream version, composite version+patch+branch+sha, or commit SHA
criteria: Composite tag format: <upstream-version>-p<N>-<branch>-<sha> (e.g., keystone:28.0.0-p0-main-a1b2c3d)
Standalone upstream version tag: <upstream-version> (e.g., keystone:28.0.0)
Standalone SHA tag: <sha> (e.g., keystone:a1b2c3d, 7 chars)
Patch count pN derived from count of .patch files in patches/<service>/<release>/ (defaults to p0 when directory absent)
Branch name sanitized for Docker tag compatibility (/ replaced with -, e.g., stable/2025.2 -> stable-2025.2)

stories.title: Smoke test validates built images
stories.want: a smoke-test job that runs keystone-manage --version against the built image and fails the workflow on non-zero exit
stories.so_that: broken images are caught before merge or registry publication
criteria: smoke-test job depends on build-service-images (needs: [build-service-images])
On PRs, smoke test uses the locally loaded image (no registry pull)
On push events, smoke test pulls the image from GHCR
docker run --rm <image> keystone-manage --version must exit 0
smoke-test job has timeout-minutes: 10

stories.title: Workflow follows repository conventions
stories.want: the build-images workflow to match ci.yaml conventions for SPDX header, quoted on key, permissions, concurrency, action pinning, and runner selection
stories.so_that: all workflows in the repository are consistent and pass REUSE compliance checks
criteria: SPDX header: '# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company' + Apache-2.0
Trigger key quoted as '"on"' to prevent YAML boolean interpretation
Top-level permissions block with contents: read and packages: write
Concurrency group: ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress on PR only
All actions pinned to full commit SHAs with version comments (e.g., actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6)
All jobs use runs-on: ubuntu-latest
GHA layer caching: cache-from: type=gha, cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

stories.title: Workflow reference documentation exists for operators and contributors
stories.want: reference documentation for the build-images workflow explaining triggers, jobs, tag schema, and PR vs push behavior
stories.so_that: I can understand how images are built, how to add new services to the matrix, and how tag derivation works
criteria: docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md created with title, quadrant, and feature metadata header
Documents all three jobs (build-base-images, build-service-images, smoke-test) with step tables
Explains tag schema with examples
Explains PR vs push behavior differences
Documents how to add a new service to the matrix

requirements.description: The workflow file SHALL include the standard SPDX header, quoted 'on' key, top-level permissions block, and concurrency control matching ci.yaml conventions
requirements.rationale: Consistency across repository workflows ensures REUSE compliance and maintainability

scenarios.name: SPDX header present
scenarios.then: the first lines contain '# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company' and '# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0'

scenarios.name: Trigger key quoted
scenarios.then: the trigger key is quoted as '"on"' (not bare 'on')

scenarios.name: Permissions block present
scenarios.then: a top-level 'permissions:' block exists with 'contents: read' and 'packages: write'

scenarios.name: Concurrency control present
scenarios.then: concurrency.group is '${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }}' and cancel-in-progress is conditional on pull_request

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL trigger on push to main and stable/** branches and on pull_request events
requirements.rationale: Images must be built for all production branches and validated on PRs before merge

scenarios.name: Push to main triggers workflow
scenarios.when: a commit is pushed to the main branch
scenarios.then: the workflow runs with all three jobs

scenarios.name: Push to stable branch triggers workflow
scenarios.when: a commit is pushed to stable/2025.2
scenarios.then: the workflow runs with all three jobs

scenarios.name: Pull request triggers workflow
scenarios.when: a pull request is opened or updated
scenarios.then: the workflow runs with PR-specific behavior (single-arch service builds, no push)

requirements.description: The build-base-images job SHALL sequentially build python-base then venv-builder, always push to GHCR (including PRs), build multi-arch, and output image refs with digest
requirements.rationale: Base images must be in GHCR for docker-image:// build-context resolution in downstream service builds; digest refs ensure reproducibility

scenarios.name: python-base built first
scenarios.then: python-base is built from images/python-base/ and pushed to ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base

scenarios.name: venv-builder built second with python-base reference
scenarios.then: venv-builder is built from images/venv-builder/ with python-base build-context override and pushed to ghcr.io/c5c3/venv-builder

scenarios.name: Base images pushed on PRs
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered by a pull_request event
scenarios.then: both base images are still pushed to GHCR (push: true regardless of event type)
and_then: service builds can resolve docker-image:// refs

scenarios.name: Job outputs contain digest refs
scenarios.then: outputs include python-base-image and venv-builder-image with full digest refs (ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base@sha256:...)

requirements.description: The build-service-images job SHALL use a matrix strategy, resolve source refs via yq, checkout upstream source, apply patches conditionally, run constraint overrides, and build with four named build-contexts
requirements.rationale: Matrix strategy enables extensibility; build-context pattern keeps Dockerfiles release-independent; conditional patches prevent failures when no patches exist

scenarios.name: Matrix over service x release
scenarios.then: it executes for each combination of service: [keystone] and release: ['2025.2']

scenarios.name: Source ref resolved from source-refs.yaml
scenarios.when: the resolve step runs for keystone/2025.2
scenarios.then: yq reads .keystone from releases/2025.2/source-refs.yaml and outputs '28.0.0'

scenarios.name: Patches applied when present
scenarios.then: git -C src/<service> apply patches/<service>/<release>/*.patch is executed

scenarios.name: Patches skipped when absent
scenarios.when: no patches/<service>/<release>/ directory exists (hashFiles is empty)
scenarios.then: the patch step is skipped via the if: condition

scenarios.name: Constraint overrides applied idempotently
scenarios.then: scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh <release> is executed (exits 0 even when no overrides exist)

scenarios.name: Four build-contexts passed to docker build
scenarios.then: build-contexts includes python-base=docker-image://..., venv-builder=docker-image://..., <service>=src/<service>, upper-constraints=releases/<release>/

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL produce three tags per service image: composite (<version>-p<N>-<branch>-<sha>), standalone upstream version (<version>), and standalone SHA (<sha>)
requirements.rationale: Multiple tags serve different use cases: composite for full traceability, version for CRD defaults, SHA for debugging

scenarios.name: Composite tag with patch count
scenarios.when: keystone 28.0.0 is built from main branch at commit a1b2c3d with no patches
scenarios.then: the composite tag is ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:28.0.0-p0-main-a1b2c3d

scenarios.name: Composite tag with patches present
scenarios.then: the patch count portion of the composite tag is p2

scenarios.name: Standalone version tag
scenarios.then: the standalone version tag is ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:28.0.0

scenarios.name: Standalone SHA tag
scenarios.when: the build runs at commit a1b2c3defgh
scenarios.then: the SHA tag is ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:a1b2c3d (7-char short SHA)

scenarios.name: Branch name sanitized
scenarios.when: the build runs on branch stable/2025.2
scenarios.then: the branch portion of the composite tag is stable-2025.2 (/ replaced with -)

requirements.description: On PR events, service images SHALL build single-arch (linux/amd64) with load: true and push: false; on push events, service images SHALL build multi-arch and push to GHCR
requirements.rationale: PR builds must be fast for developer iteration; push builds must produce multi-arch production images

scenarios.name: PR builds single-arch with local load
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered by pull_request
scenarios.then: service images build with platforms: linux/amd64, load: true, push: false
and_then: image is available locally for smoke test via docker run

scenarios.name: Push builds multi-arch and pushes
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered by push to main
scenarios.then: service images build with platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64, push: true
and_then: image is available in GHCR for smoke test via docker pull

scenarios.name: Tags only applied on push
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered by pull_request
scenarios.then: no tags are pushed to GHCR for service images (only base images receive tags on PRs)

requirements.description: The smoke-test job SHALL run keystone-manage --version against the built image and fail the workflow on non-zero exit
requirements.rationale: Smoke test catches broken images before merge or registry publication

scenarios.name: Smoke test passes on valid image
scenarios.when: a correctly built keystone image is available
scenarios.then: docker run --rm <image> keystone-manage --version exits 0

scenarios.name: Smoke test fails on broken image
scenarios.when: the built image has a broken keystone installation
scenarios.then: docker run --rm <image> keystone-manage --version exits non-zero

scenarios.name: PR smoke test uses local image
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered by pull_request
scenarios.then: the smoke test uses the locally loaded image (no docker pull needed)

scenarios.name: Push smoke test pulls from GHCR
scenarios.when: the workflow is triggered by push
scenarios.then: the smoke test pulls the image from GHCR before running

requirements.description: All third-party GitHub Actions SHALL be pinned to full commit SHAs with version comments
requirements.rationale: SHA pinning prevents supply-chain attacks via tag mutation; version comments aid maintainability and Renovate tracking

scenarios.name: Actions pinned to SHAs
scenarios.then: every uses: directive references a full 40-character commit SHA (e.g., actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd)

scenarios.name: Version comments present
scenarios.then: every SHA-pinned action has a comment with the version tag (e.g., # v6)

scenarios.name: actions/checkout matches ci.yaml
scenarios.when: the workflow file is compared to ci.yaml
scenarios.then: the same commit SHA is used for actions/checkout (de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6)

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL use GHA layer caching for Docker builds via cache-from: type=gha and cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
requirements.rationale: Layer caching reduces build times significantly for repeated builds, especially for base images that change infrequently

scenarios.name: Cache configuration present on all build steps
scenarios.then: every docker/build-push-action step includes cache-from: type=gha and cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

scenarios.name: Subsequent builds are faster
scenarios.when: the workflow runs after a previous successful build
scenarios.then: unchanged layers are pulled from GHA cache instead of being rebuilt

requirements.description: Reference documentation SHALL be created for the build-images workflow covering all jobs, tag schema, PR vs push behavior, and extensibility
requirements.rationale: Contributors need to understand the workflow to add new services, debug build failures, and understand the tag derivation logic

scenarios.name: Documentation file exists
scenarios.then: the file exists with YAML frontmatter (title, quadrant, feature) matching the existing docs/reference/ convention

scenarios.name: All jobs documented
scenarios.then: build-base-images, build-service-images, and smoke-test jobs are each documented with step tables

scenarios.name: Tag schema documented with examples
scenarios.then: the tag derivation logic is explained with concrete examples (e.g., keystone:28.0.0-p0-main-a1b2c3d)

tasks.title: Create workflow skeleton with build-base-images job (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-008, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` with: SPDX header matching ci.yaml, `name: Build Images`, quoted `"on"` key with triggers (push to main and stable/**, pull_request), top-level permissions (contents: read, packages: write), concurrency control matching ci.yaml pattern. Add the `build-base-images` job with: runs-on: ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes: 30, steps for checkout (SHA-pinned), docker/setup-buildx-action (SHA-pinned), docker/login-action to GHCR (SHA-pinned), two sequential docker/build-push-action steps — first for python-base (context: images/python-base, platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64, push: true always, GHA caching), then for venv-builder (context: images/venv-builder, build-contexts: python-base=docker-image://ghcr.io/c5c3/python-base@<digest>, platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64, push: true always, GHA caching). Job outputs: python-base-image and venv-builder-image with digest refs from build-push-action outputs. Pin actions/checkout to the same SHA as ci.yaml (de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6). Look up and pin docker/setup-buildx-action, docker/login-action, docker/build-push-action to their latest v3/v3/v6 release SHAs respectively.

tasks.title: Add build-service-images job with matrix, source resolution, patches, and constraint overrides (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Add `build-service-images` job to the workflow with: needs: [build-base-images], runs-on: ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes: 30, strategy.matrix with service: [keystone] and release: ['2025.2']. Steps: (1) Checkout C5C3 repo (SHA-pinned actions/checkout). (2) Resolve source ref: `ref=$(yq '."${{ matrix.service }}"' releases/${{ matrix.release }}/source-refs.yaml)` → output to GITHUB_OUTPUT. (3) Checkout openstack/<service> at resolved ref into src/<service> (second actions/checkout with repository and path). (4) Apply patches: conditional on `hashFiles(format('patches/{0}/{1}/*.patch', matrix.service, matrix.release)) != ''`, run `git -C src/${{ matrix.service }} apply patches/${{ matrix.service }}/${{ matrix.release }}/*.patch`. (5) Apply constraint overrides: run `scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh ${{ matrix.release }}`.

tasks.title: Add tag derivation step and image build with build-contexts and PR/push conditional (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Continue the build-service-images job by adding: (6) Tag derivation step that reads upstream version from the already-resolved source ref, counts .patch files in patches/<service>/<release>/ (defaulting to 0 if directory absent), sanitizes branch name (${GITHUB_REF_NAME//\//-}), extracts 7-char short SHA (${GITHUB_SHA::7}), and assembles three tags: composite `<version>-p<N>-<branch>-<sha>`, standalone `<version>`, standalone `<sha>`. Output tags as GITHUB_OUTPUT. (7) docker/setup-buildx-action (SHA-pinned). (8) docker/login-action to GHCR (SHA-pinned). (9) docker/build-push-action with: context: images/${{ matrix.service }}, file: images/${{ matrix.service }}/Dockerfile, build-contexts (python-base=docker-image://...@digest, venv-builder=docker-image://...@digest from job outputs, <service>=src/<service>, upper-constraints=releases/<release>/), platforms conditional (linux/amd64 on PR, linux/amd64,linux/arm64 on push), load conditional (true on PR, false on push), push conditional (false on PR, true on push), tags from derivation step (only on push), GHA caching. Job outputs the image ref for the smoke test.

tasks.title: Add smoke-test job (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Add `smoke-test` job to the workflow with: needs: [build-service-images], runs-on: ubuntu-latest, timeout-minutes: 10. The job needs to handle two scenarios: (a) On PRs, the image was loaded locally in the service build job — but since smoke-test runs on a different runner, the PR path needs to use the same build-service-images runner or re-build. Actually, GHA jobs run on separate runners, so for PRs the smoke test needs the image. The standard pattern is: on push, pull from GHCR; on PRs, the image needs to be available. Since service images aren't pushed on PRs, the smoke test step should be part of the build-service-images job for PRs, or use a different approach. Implement: add a smoke-test step directly within build-service-images (after the build step, conditional: always run `docker run --rm <local-image> keystone-manage --version` when load: true). Also add a separate smoke-test job for push events that pulls from GHCR and runs the same command. This handles both cases correctly.

tasks.title: Create workflow validation test script (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007, REQ-008, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create `tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh` following the existing test script pattern (SPDX header, source assertions.sh, PASS/FAIL counters). Tests: (1) SPDX header present in first 3 lines. (2) Quoted 'on' key present. (3) Top-level permissions block with contents: read and packages: write. (4) Concurrency group matches pattern. (5) Three jobs defined: build-base-images, build-service-images, smoke-test. (6) build-service-images has needs: containing build-base-images. (7) Matrix includes service and release keys. (8) All 'uses:' lines contain @ followed by 40-char hex SHA. (9) All 'uses:' lines with SHA have a version comment (# vN). (10) cache-from: type=gha present in build steps. (11) push trigger includes main and stable/**. (12) pull_request trigger present. (13) timeout-minutes defined on all jobs. (14) runs-on: ubuntu-latest on all jobs. Use yq for YAML parsing where possible, grep for pattern matching. Requires: yq.

tasks.title: Create reference documentation for build-images workflow (REQ-010)
tasks.description: Create `docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md` with YAML frontmatter (title: Build Images Workflow, quadrant: infrastructure, feature: CC-0007) matching the convention in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md. Sections: File Location, Trigger Events (table: push main+stable/**, pull_request), Permissions (contents: read, packages: write with explanation), Jobs (build-base-images with step table, build-service-images with step table including matrix explanation, smoke-test with step table), Tag Schema (table showing all three tag formats with examples), PR vs Push Behavior (comparison table showing platforms, push, load, tags differences), Concurrency, SPDX Header, GHA Caching, How to Add a New Service (numbered steps: add Dockerfile, add to source-refs.yaml, add to matrix), Dependencies on CC-0006 (table listing Dockerfiles, release config, scripts used by the workflow).

test_specifications.expected: First 3 lines should contain SPDX-FileCopyrightText and SPDX-License-Identifier matching ci.yaml
The trigger key should be quoted as '"on"' in the YAML file
Top-level permissions block should contain contents: read and packages: write
Concurrency group should match '${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }}' pattern
Push trigger should include main and stable/** branches

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline builds base images as prerequisites for service images
test_specifications.expected: Workflow should define build-base-images, build-service-images, and smoke-test jobs

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline builds base images as prerequisites for service images
test_specifications.expected: build-service-images should have needs: containing build-base-images

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline builds service images with matrix strategy
test_specifications.expected: build-service-images strategy.matrix should include service and release keys
Every uses: directive should reference a 40-character hex commit SHA
Every SHA-pinned action should have a version comment (# vN)
cache-from: type=gha should be present in docker/build-push-action steps
All jobs should have timeout-minutes defined
All jobs should use runs-on: ubuntu-latest

review_criteria: All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-010) have corresponding test coverage in verify_build_images_workflow.sh
Workflow file validates as correct YAML (yq . .github/workflows/build-images.yaml exits 0)
All actions pinned to full 40-character commit SHAs with version comments matching ci.yaml convention
SPDX header, quoted on key, permissions block, and concurrency control match ci.yaml conventions exactly
Tag derivation logic correctly handles: no patches dir (p0), empty patches dir (p0), branch name with slashes (sanitized to dashes), 7-char short SHA
PR vs push behavior correctly differentiates: single-arch/load/no-push on PRs vs multi-arch/push on push events
build-base-images always pushes to GHCR (even on PRs) and outputs digest refs consumed by build-service-images
Smoke test job correctly runs keystone-manage --version and fails workflow on non-zero exit
Reference documentation at docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md follows existing convention (YAML frontmatter, step tables, examples)
implementation_notes:
This feature creates a single GitHub Actions workflow file (.github/workflows/build-images.yaml) plus validation tests and reference documentation. No backend, frontend, or API changes.

**Architectural decisions:**
- Base images always pushed to GHCR (even on PRs) because docker-image:// build-context refs require registry availability. This is a deliberate trade-off: small base images are pushed on every PR to enable the docker-image:// pattern that keeps Dockerfiles registry-independent.
- Smoke test on PRs must run within the build-service-images job (as an additional step after the build) because --load makes the image available only on the same runner. A separate smoke-test job runs on push events pulling from GHCR.
- Tag derivation is done in a shell step rather than a reusable action for simplicity and transparency.
- The matrix currently has a single entry (keystone x 2025.2) but is structured for easy extension.

**Action SHA pinning:**
- actions/checkout: de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6 (from ci.yaml)
- docker/setup-buildx-action, docker/login-action, docker/build-push-action: Look up latest v3/v3/v6 release SHAs from their GitHub repos at implementation time. The exact SHAs must be current when the PR is created.

**Key files to reference during implementation:**
- .github/workflows/ci.yaml — convention reference for SPDX, permissions, concurrency, action pinning
- images/python-base/Dockerfile, images/venv-builder/Dockerfile, images/keystone/Dockerfile — build targets
- releases/2025.2/source-refs.yaml — source ref config (keystone: '28.0.0')
- scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh — invoked during service builds
- tests/container-images/verify_spdx_headers.sh, tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh — test script conventions to follow
- tests/lib/assertions.sh — shared assertion helpers
- docs/reference/ci-workflow.md — documentation convention to follow

**Potential pitfalls:**
- The venv-builder Dockerfile currently uses `FROM python-base` (local tag). In CI, this resolves via the --build-context python-base=docker-image://... override. Ensure the build-context name matches exactly.
- hashFiles() in GHA evaluates at job setup time, not step time. The conditional for patches must use the correct format() syntax.
- GHA cache keys for Docker layers are per-workflow, so base and service images share the GHA cache namespace — this is fine since BuildKit uses content-addressed layers.
- On PRs, docker/build-push-action with load: true only supports single-platform builds. Multi-platform + load is not supported.
